competition.narrative: Competition confirmed: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and sole named competitor. Competitive position: Ariba is the system being replaced, not a live threat in the RFP. Omar (Director, Group Procurement) stated Ariba is 'unintuitive and cumbersome, requiring extensive training and causing low user adoption', and post-SAP-acquisition it has seen 'reduced innovation and increasing licensing fees without enhanced functionality' (Avoma 2025-10-14). Ariba contract ended December 2025 (original timeline), and Omar confirmed on 2025-10-14 that they are 'unhappy Ariba customer looking to replace quickly'. Ariba sourcing modules (RFPs/auctions) used for 4-5 years but underutilized overall. Ariba is not defending or competing actively per available evidence; no Ariba retention offer or engagement mentioned. Wedge: Zycus wins on AI depth (Merlin autonomous negotiation, AI-driven intake, ANA), unified home-grown S2P platform with consistent UX vs Ariba's complexity, proven multi-ERP integration including SAP ECC, and phased low-risk approach. Differentiation stressed on both calls. Risk: October 2026 formal RFP will open the field to Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, and potentially a heavily discounted Ariba retention offer from the relevant stakeholder lock, quantified ROI, and a winning budgetary proposal before October, the RFP becomes an open race where Zycus's early influence advantage erodes. How we win: (1) Lock the budgetary proposal and quantified ROI with Omar before RFP launch to anchor the business case. (2) Map and engage the CFO/board sponsor to secure executive-level advocacy. (3) Emphasize Merlin AI and autonomous negotiation as the centerpiece differentiator in Phase 2 expansion, making it the multi-year strategic platform vs point solutions. (4) Confirm SAP ECC connector availability immediately to eliminate integration risk. (5) Offer onsite workshops in Kuwait (if travel viable post-conflict) to deepen champion strength and multi-thread further before RFP.

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria confirmed from BRD response, demo sessions, and buyer questions. Alghanim requires: (1) Full-cycle S2P/P2P from requisition through sourcing (RFPs/auctions) and contract award in Phase 1, explicitly excluding GR, invoicing, e-payables initially (Omar: 'Initial deployment will cover requisition through contract award—specifically RFPs and auctions for 30–40% of indirect spend—while invoicing and payment modules will be deferred', 2025-10-14 call). (2) Sourcing replacement (RFPs/auctions) is 'definitely mandatory' to replace Ariba (Avoma 2025-10-14). (3) Ease of use and intuitive UX vs Ariba, minimal training, easy workflow changes (Omar repeatedly cited poor Ariba UX as pain, 2025-10-14). (4) Single procurement platform integrating with multiple SAP ECC instances and business-specific ERPs as single source of truth (Omar, 2025-10-14). (5) Phased, low-risk implementation: start with minimum viable P2P scope and expand safely over 3 years (Omar: 'Three-year transformation vision to expand into e-contracts, e-payments and advanced AI modules; phase 1 to deliver minimal viable P2P', 2025-10-14). (6) Strong AI capabilities, especially Merlin AI for sourcing, autonomous negotiation, and intake (Omar requested AI-focused demo and Zycus emphasized Merlin depth on both calls). (7) Category-specific supplier onboarding, role-based access control inherited from SAP/HR, configurable GRN fields, and API-based two-way SAP integration (buyer questions on 2026-04-23 demo). (8) Ability to adapt to multiple diverse business lines: automotive, manufacturing, retail, franchises (Next_Step__c). Zycus positioned strongly on AI depth, unified S2P platform UX, proven multi-ERP integrations, and phased approach. SAP ECC connector confirmation pending is a criteria risk.

Forward path (usage numbers → budgetary estimate → RFP October → vendor selection → contracting/legal) cannot complete by 2026-12-31. Date will slip to Q4 2026 or Q1 2027.

Economic Buyer unmapped and unengaged — Omar (Decision Maker) is active champion-in-development with strong access, but the CFO or C-level sponsor who will approve the commercial investment is not identified or engaged. Recommended move: have Omar introduce Zycus to the EB/CFO during the budgetary estimate stage (end June 2026) to validate budget authority and secure executive sponsorship before the October 2026 RFP launch opens the field to competitors. Without EB lock, the deal risks stalling at commercial approval or losing to a competitor with C-level relationships.

Business case not quantified — buyer articulated clear operational pain (Ariba poor UX, low adoption, no unified spend visibility, rising fees) but no dollar savings target, ROI figure, or cost-of-doing-nothing. The budgetary estimate exercise (usage numbers due end June 2026) is the opportunity to co-develop a quantified value case with Omar that ties Zycus capabilities (AI negotiation, Merlin, spend consolidation, Ariba license recapture) to measurable financial impact. Recommended move: during budgetary estimate stage, work with Omar to model savings by category (tail-spend optimization via Merlin negotiation, PPV reduction via spend analytics, Ariba license savings, faster cycle time) and build an ROI case he can take to the EB/CFO. A quantified business case is the wedge that makes Zycus the obvious choice before the RFP scoring begins.

October 2026 RFP will open the competitive field — SAP Ariba is the only named competitor today (incumbent, weak on merit, buyer motivated to switch), but the formal RFP launch in October 2026 will likely introduce other SaaS vendors (Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer) into the shortlist. Zycus has early influence via BRD response and successful two-hour demo (2026-04-23), but must convert that lead into a preferred-vendor lock before the RFP becomes an open race. Recommended move: use the budgetary estimate stage (end June 2026) and the 4-month runway to October to deliver a winning, BRD-aligned proposal, secure EB sponsorship, quantify the ROI, confirm SAP ECC integration, and position Merlin/AI as the strategic differentiator no other vendor can match — making the RFP a formality rather than a competitive bake-off. Ask Omar directly: how can Zycus win the RFP, what does the evaluation committee care most about, and who else will be in the shortlist. Then execute against that intel.

Recommended move: during budgetary estimate stage, work with Omar to model savings by category (tail-spend optimization via Merlin negotiation, PPV reduction via spend analytics, Ariba license savings, faster cycle time) and build an ROI case he can take to the EB/CFO. A quantified business case is the wedge that makes Zycus the obvious choice before the RFP scoring begins.

Zycus has early influence via BRD response and successful two-hour demo (2026-04-23), but must convert that lead into a preferred-vendor lock before the RFP becomes an open race. Recommended move: use the budgetary estimate stage (end June 2026) and the 4-month runway to October to deliver a winning, BRD-aligned proposal, secure EB sponsorship, quantify the ROI, confirm SAP ECC integration, and position Merlin/AI as the strategic differentiator no other vendor can match — making the RFP a formality rather than a competitive bake-off. Ask Omar directly: how can Zycus win the RFP, what does the evaluation committee care most about, and who else will be in the shortlist. Then execute against that intel.
